data.title: JPMorgan warns about tariffs: ‘We do not think that we are out of the woods on this front’
data.description:
JPMorgan’s global market strategy team indicated on Monday morning that equity markets for the most part have shrugged off tariff concerns, but said “we do not think that we are out of the woods on this front.”

The asset manager further emphasized that the ongoing uncertainty surrounding trade policy, which has significantly increased since the election of Donald Trump late last year, is unlikely to be resolved in the near future.

“We see tariff headline news flow risk as a continuous theme through this year, and beyond. If the trade war escalates, business confidence generally will be hit, but the US is likely to be less affected than others,” JPMorgan went on to state.

While not directly mentioned, investors looking to keep a close eye on the international trade and tariff pulse may look towards exchange traded funds that provide focus on nations and regions such as China, Canada, Mexico, Europe, and broader emerging market nations. See below a grouping of funds for investors below:

China ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

Canada ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FXC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA), and (BATS:BBCA). Mexico ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWW), (NYSEARCA:FLMX), and (NYSEARCA:MEXX).

Euro Zone ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VGK), (BATS:EZU), IEUR), (BATS:BBEU), and (NYSEARCA:FEZ).

Emerging Market ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VWO), (NYSEARCA:IEMG), (NYSEARCA:EEM), (NYSEARCA:SPEM), SCHE), and (NYSEARCA:AVEM).

data.title: Robotaxi pure play WeRide soars after landing key Beijing approval
data.description:
WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) announced on Monday that it landed approval to launch its latest generation robotaxi, the GXR, for fully unmanned paid ride-hailing services in Beijing. The company noted the GXR marks WeRide's (NASDAQ:WRD) second robotaxi model to achieve fully driverless commercial operations in Beijing and is GXR's first large-scale commercial deployment in China, following a launch on the Uber (UBER) platform in Abu Dhabi last December.

The robotaxi service area spans key regions within the Beijing Economic-Technological Development Area, including high-speed railway stations. GXR is also approved for commercial operations on highways, including routes to and from Beijing Daxing International Airport.

In just four months since WeRide (WRD) officially launched GXR, the company has rapidly progressed to fully unmanned commercial operations, which is said demonstrates "WeRide speed" in the commercial deployment of autonomous driving.

The culmination of over five years of WeRide's (WRD) dedication to Robotaxi, GXR features industry-leading L4-level redundant drive-by-wire chassis architecture, open mobile travel space, and the world's first hidden B-pillar design. It can accommodate up to five passengers, offering the widest entry and exit space, the best seating space, and the largest luggage storage space in the Robotaxi industry. Users can hail a GXR on WeRide Go app, available in major mobile app stores.

"The commercial launch of Robotaxi GXR in Beijing represents a pivotal achievement for WeRide and the autonomous driving industry," said Dr. Tony Han, founder and CEO of WeRide. "By advancing from product launch to unmanned commercial operations in just four months, we’ve demonstrated that autonomous technology is ready to meet various transportation needs at scale. With this expansion, we continue to set the standard for autonomous mobility, making self-driving technology a reality for passengers everywhere," he added.

Notably, WeRide (WRD) has launched robotaxi services in Beijing, Guangzhou, Nanjing, Ordos, Suzhou, and Abu Dhabi. In 2025, WeRide (WRD) will launch fully unmanned Robotaxi operations in Zurich, Switzerland. With the commercial launch of GXR, WeRide (WRD) will continue to expand the service scope and scale, planning to increase the number of Robotaxi operations in Beijing to several hundred by 2025

Shares of WeRide (WRD) soared 14% in premarket trading on Monday. The autonomous vehicle stock is now up more than 80% on a year-to-date basis. The market cap on WeRide (WRD) is up to $6.7 billion.

data.title: Axsome migraine drug Symbravo achieves goal in late-stage trial
data.description:
• Axome Theapeutics (NASDAQ:AXSM) said that a phase 3 trial of its migraine drug candidate Symbravo, a combination of meloxicam and rizatriptan, met its primary endpoint.
  • Patients taking Symbravo saw greater migraine treatment response compared to when they were on an oral CGRP inhibitor, as measured by the Migraine Treatment Optimization Questionnaire (mTOQ-4). CGRP inhibitors, such as Pfizer's (PFE) Nurtec ODC (rimegepant) and AbbVie's (ABBV) Quilipta (atogepant) are considered first-line migraine treatments.
  • When on Symbravo, patients had a mTOQ-4 total score of 5.2 versus 2.8 when they were just taking a CGRP inhibitor.
  • The phase 3 open-label trial, EMERGE, enrolled patients who were taking a CGRP inhibitor for at least one month and had an inadequate reponse. Patients were switched for their next four attacks.

data.title: Starbucks announces 1,100 job cuts
data.description:
Coffee shops giant Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) announced on Monday plans to eliminate 1,100 current support partner roles.

Additionally, the company is also eliminating several hundred open and unfilled positions.

SBUX shares were trading -0.01% pre-market at $111.74

CEO Brian Niccol had said in a January letter to employees that the company's strategy reset will involve difficult decisions and choices, including some job eliminations and smaller support teams moving forward.

The individual partners whose jobs are being eliminated will be informed by mid-day tomorrow.

"We will share more on the timeline for this week and how we are taking care of partners shortly," said Niccol in a message to all global support partners, communicating decisions specific to non-retail, support partners.

Other changes to be made to the partner roles, including responsibilities and reporting structures, are set to be shared with affected partners by the end of the week.

The decision does not affect in-store teams or the investments to be made in store hours.

data.title: SEC closes Robinhood Crypto probe with no action
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) stock climbed 2.4% in Monday premarket trading after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission closed its investigation into Robinhood Crypto without taking any enforcement action, the company said on Monday.

The company received a letter, dated Feb. 21, from the regulator advising it of the probe's conclusion. Last May, Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) received a Wells Notice, which notifies a company when the SEC intends to proceed with enforcement actions.

"Let me be crystal clear — this investigation never should have been opened," Dan Gallagher chief legal, compliance and corporate affairs officer at Robinhood, said. "Robinhood Crypto always has and will always respect federal securities laws and never allowed transactions in securities."

He added: "We appreciate the formal closing of this investigation, and we are happy to see a return to the rule of law and commitment to fairness at the SEC."

Robinhood (HOOD) repeated that it has been selective in the crypto tokens it has allowed its users to trade, and chose not to provide certain services and products that the SEC under previous Chair Gary Gensler alleged are securities.

"We’re helping advance a more transparent and tailored regulatory environment for digital assets, and look forward to working with the SEC under this new administration to establish a clear path forward," Robinhood said in a statement.

data.title: First Nation launches legal action against McEwen Mining, claims breached agreement
data.description:
The Apitipi Anicinapek Nation has filed a lawsuit against McEwen Mining (NYSE:MUX), alleging the company has breached its impact benefit agreement related to the Black Fox project, Mining Weekly reported Monday.

The lawsuit, launched early this month in Ontario Superior Court, claims McEwen (NYSE:MUX) has failed to meet its financial commitments and has caused environmental harm on the tribe's territory.

AAN Chief June Black said McEwen (MUX) has failed to make yearly compensation payments in shares to the First Nation for more than a decade and had allowed tailings leakages that harm fish and wildlife.

McEwen (MUX) denied the allegations that it has let waste materials seep into a nearby creek, as alleged in the lawsuit.

"Our tailings facility is operated under an approval issued by the Ontario Ministry of Environment, Conservation and Parks, and is subject to frequent inspections by representatives from a variety of provincial and federal ministries," the company said.

McEwen (MUX) also said the obligation to provide payments of 25K Brigus common shares annually predated its acquisition of the Black Fox mine.

data.title: Freshpet jumps as Jefferies upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Freshpet (NASDAQ:FRPT) gained over 6% in pre-market on Monday as Jefferies upgraded the American pet food company shares to Buy from Hold, citing that its valuation is at a five-year low, and its shares are worth 50% above where it is trading.

However, Jefferies analysts reduced the price target to $150 from $155.

Despite a 22% increase in sales, 68% surge in adjusted EBITDA, and 650 basis point expansion in adjusted gross margin, the company missed Q4 Wall Street estimates, resulting in shares dwindled over 18.50% on last Thursday.

For FY25, the company forecasts sales to be between $1.18B and $1.21B, up from $975.2M in 2024, but less than $1.22B estimates.

Freshpet's shares are down 32% this year despite strong Q4 results and a promising outlook, noted Jefferies analyst Kaumil Gajrawala.

Gajrawala added, “Drivers like pet premiumization remain strong, and we think the overall pet industry is improving. Sales should compound 23% to '27, while expanding margins. A rare combination.”

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, FRPT has a Hold rating, with A+ in the prospective of growth, while the rating was dragged down due to D- in momentum and D in profitability.

Seeking Alpha analysts echoed the same sentiment and rated the stock as Hold.

However, Wall Street analysts are bullish and rated the company as a Buy.

data.title: Former Statera CEO Michael Handley named Tivic Biopharma president
data.description:
• Tivic Health Systems (NASDAQ:TIVC) has announced that Michael K. Handley, formerly CEO, President and Chairman of Statera Biopharma, has joined Tivic Health as the President of Tivic Biopharma, a newly formed division of Tivic Health.
  • Additionally, he will assume the role of COO of Tivic Health and will work closely with the CEO and other leadership team members to develop and operationalize company-wide strategies.

data.title: Patrick Industries closes on deal for Medallion Instrumentation Systems
data.description:
Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK) announced on Monday that it closed on its acquisition of Medallion Instrumentation Systems on Monday. The deal price for the premier provider of customized instrumentation and vehicle electronics for marine, RV, powersports, on-highway and military markets was undisclosed.

Medallion's engineering team was highlighted as providing complete systems solutions that cater to customer needs, including digital switching, lighting controls, integrated audio, sensor products, wire harnessing, gauges and LCD touchscreen displays. Medallion's 2024 revenue was approximately $38 million.

"We are thrilled to welcome the entire Medallion team to the Patrick family as we advance our mission of providing full-solutions packages to our customers," said Andy Nemeth, Chief Executive Officer of Patrick.

"With the growing customer preference towards digital switching, custom LCD displays, gauges and controllers in the Outdoor Enthusiast space, this acquisition purposefully connects our complement of existing product offerings into a complete technology solution that can be customized to our customer's vision and needs," noted Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK) CEO Andy Nemeth. "In tandem with our strong mechanical expertise and customer relationships, Medallion's engineering, design, and sales capabilities offer an exciting opportunity to further expand our presence in the marine, powersports, and RV markets." he added.

Following its absorption by Patrick Industries (PATK), Medallion will continue to operate under its existing brand name and within its current facility.

Shares of Patrick Industries (PATK) are up 8.6% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Strategy buys 20.4K bitcoin last week
data.description:
• Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) resumed its bitcoin purchases and acquired 20.4K bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the week ended February 23, the company said in a filing.
  • The firm said it paid ~$1.99B in cash at an average price of ~$97,514 per bitcoin in the period between February 18 and February 23.
  • The purchases were made using proceeds from the convertible notes offering.
  • As of February 23, the firm, along with its units, held an aggregate of ~499,096 bitcoins, which were acquired at an aggregate purchase price of ~$33.1 billion.
  • Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) had not acquired any bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the period between Feb. 10 and 17.
